PatientProfile.title: PatientProfile
PatientProfile.description: Represents the personal information of a patient identified by their unique archive number. This entity stores core demographic and contact details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PatientProfile entity, corresponding to the patient's archive number. (Primary Key)

name.description: The full name of the patient.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the patient.

MedicalAnomalyRecord.title: MedicalAnomalyRecord
MedicalAnomalyRecord.description: Records details of a significant abnormal result found during a medical checkup, including notification and suggested actions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicalAnomalyRecord entity. (Primary Key)

patientProfileId.description: Reference to the PatientProfile entity's ID (archiveNumber). (Relationship: PatientProfile 1:N MedicalAnomalyRecord)

checkupNumber.description: The 12-digit checkup identification number, where the first 8 digits represent the date (YYYYMMDD).

checkupDate.description: The date when the medical checkup was performed.

notifiedPerson.description: The name of the person who was notified about the anomaly.

anomalyCategory.description: The category of the important anomaly result (A or B). A: requires immediate clinical intervention. B: requires further clinical examination or treatment.

anomalyDetails.description: Detailed description of the important anomaly result.

disposalSuggestions.description: Suggestions or recommendations for the disposal or management of the anomaly.

isNotified.description: Indicates whether the notified person has been informed about the anomaly. Defaults to true.

isHealthEducationProvided.description: Indicates whether health education has been provided regarding the anomaly. Defaults to true.

notifier.description: The name of the user who performed the notification (usually the system logged-in user).

notifiedPersonFeedback.description: Feedback received from the notified person regarding the anomaly.

notificationDate.description: The date when the notification was made.

notificationTime.description: The time when the notification was made.

MedicalReportFile.title: MedicalReportFile
MedicalReportFile.description: Stores metadata and links for uploaded PDF medical reports associated with a specific anomaly record.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicalReportFile entity. (Primary Key)

medicalAnomalyRecordId.description: Reference to the MedicalAnomalyRecord entity's ID to which this report belongs. (Relationship: MedicalAnomalyRecord 1:N MedicalReportFile)

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded report file.

filePath.description: The internal path or URI where the report file is stored.

checkDate.description: The date indicated on the medical report itself, representing when the checkup for this report occurred.

fileCategory.description: The category of the uploaded file, e.g., '体检报告' (Medical Report).

definition.description: Collection for storing user roles. The existence of a document with a user's ID (`userId`) in this collection indicates they are a medical staff member with access privileges to patient data. This is used for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the medical staff member, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Top-level collection for storing essential demographic and contact information for each patient. Access is granted to all authenticated medical staff.

params.name: patientProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the PatientProfile, corresponding to the patient's archive number.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific patient profile, storing details of significant abnormal medical checkup results. Each record includes a denormalized `patientProfileId` for authorization independence and data integrity, ensuring it links correctly to its parent patient. Access is granted to all authenticated medical staff.

params.name: patientProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent PatientProfile.

params.name: medicalAnomalyRecordId
params.description: The unique identifier for the MedicalAnomalyRecord.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific medical anomaly record, storing metadata and links to uploaded PDF medical reports. Each file record includes a denormalized `medicalAnomalyRecordId` for authorization independence and data integrity. Access is granted to all authenticated medical staff.

params.name: patientProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent PatientProfile.

params.name: medicalAnomalyRecordId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent MedicalAnomalyRecord.

params.name: medicalReportFileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the MedicalReportFile.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support a "Medical Anomaly Result Management System" by following core design principles for security, scalability, and debuggability. The primary authorization model assumes a role-based access for all medical staff, meaning any authenticated medical staff user can access and manage all patient data.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This structure explicitly avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies that would require `get()` calls in security rules. Instead, access is determined by checking if the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) is a recognized `medicalStaff` member via a simple `exists()` check on a dedicated `/roles_medicalStaff` collection. For subcollections, the parent IDs (`patientProfileId`, `medicalAnomalyRecordId`) are inherently part of the document path, ensuring contextual integrity without additional `get()` operations. Data denormalization for authorization attributes is not strictly necessary in this model, as access is broadly granted to all staff, rather than specific per-patient or per-record ownership.

**Clarity of Intent (Debuggability):** The collection paths are highly semantic and mirror the logical relationships between entities. For example, anomaly records for a patient are nested under that patient's profile: `/patientProfiles/{patientProfileId}/medicalAnomalyRecords/{medicalAnomalyRecordId}`. This makes the data hierarchy transparent and directly informs security rule construction, making authorization logic easy to understand and debug.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims):** User roles are managed within the database using a dedicated `/roles_medicalStaff/{userId}` collection. The mere existence of a document with the user's UID in this collection grants them `medicalStaff` permissions, which can then be checked using `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_medicalStaff/$(request.auth.uid))`. This adheres to the principle of Database-Backed Access Control.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure inherently supports secure `list` operations. Since `medicalStaff` have broad access, a `list` operation on `/patientProfiles` will return all patient profiles that the authorized user can access. Similarly, `list` operations on `/patientProfiles/{patientProfileId}/medicalAnomalyRecords` will securely return all anomaly records for a *specific* patient profile, because the `isMedicalStaff()` check applies consistently. The rules ensure that requests are validated against the requested path segments, preventing unauthorized filtering or data exposure.

**Invariants:** The hierarchical path structure helps maintain data integrity and ownership relationships. For instance, a `MedicalAnomalyRecord` implicitly belongs to a `PatientProfile` via its path, and corresponding `patientProfileId` in the document ensures consistency. This makes it straightforward to enforce rules like "a new anomaly record's `patientProfileId` must match the `patientProfileId` in its path."

**Structural Segregation:** Each collection (`/patientProfiles`, `/medicalAnomalyRecords`, `/medicalReportFiles`) holds documents of a homogeneous security posture (all accessible by medical staff), simplifying security rules significantly. There is no mixing of public/private or different permission levels within a single collection.

**Access Modeling:** Path-based ownership is used for `PatientProfile`, and then extended hierarchically for `MedicalAnomalyRecord` and `MedicalReportFile`, aligning with the 1:N relationships. This is the most secure and debuggable pattern for nested, user-associated data in Firestore.
